title: Stella Yu | EECS at UC Berkeley
content: Stella Yu | EECS at UC Berkeley Stella Yu: Faculty Home Page Stella Yu Adjunct Professor Contact Information stellayu@berkeley.edu Links Personal Homepage Publications Dissertations Research Support Judy Tam 676 Soda 4971370 j_tam@berkeley.edu Biography Stella Yu received her doctorate from the School of Computer Science at Carnegie Mellon University, where she studied robotics at the Robotics Institute and vision science at the Center for the Neural Basis of Cognition. She continued her computer vision research as a postdoctoral fellow at UC Berkeley, and then studied art and vision as a Clare Booth Luce Professor at Boston College, during which time she received an NSF CAREER award. Dr. Yu is the ICSI Vision Group Director and a BIDS Senior Fellow. Her group studies visual perception from multiple perspectives: human vision, computer vision, robotic vision, and artistic vision. Publications Education 2003, Ph.D., Robotics, Carnegie Mellon University Research Areas Artificial Intelligence (AI) Control, Intelligent Systems, and Robotics (CIR) Graphics (GR) Signal Processing (SP) Computer Vision Research Centers Berkeley Deep Drive (BDD) Berkeley Institute for Data Science (BIDS) FHL Vive Center for Enhanced Reality International Computer Science Institute (ICSI)
